title: Chinas Year End Crude Quota Release
section_path: Chinas Year End Crude Quota Release
section_path_text: Chinas Year End Crude Quota Release
summary:
Main
Behind China's year-end crude quotas: What they reveal - and how they reshape sanctioned barrels
By Emma Li
China issued its final batch of crude import quotas last week, totalling more than 7.5mt - notably higher than the 6mt released in the same batch last year. While some may view this as a sign of policy easing, the increase is better understood through the mechanics of China's quota system and ongoing structural tightness. Under China's planned framework, all refiners except the five major state-owned companies must hold import quotas to bring in seaborne crude.
